questions.question_text_full:
Parts of the following sentence are given as options. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error.
She has been working with me on this project from two years.

Select the most appropriate direct form of the given sentence.
She exclaimed that I looked very beautiful in that dress.

options.text: She said, “How beautiful you look in this dress!”
She said, “How beautiful I look in this dress!”
She said, “How beautiful she looks in this dress!”
She said, “How beautiful I looked in that dress!”

questions.correct_option_text: She said, “How beautiful you look in this dress!”

questions.question_text_full:
Select the most appropriate direct form of the given sentence.
Anurag says that he would like to be an electronics engineer when he grows up.

options.text: Anurag says, “He likes to be an electronics engineer when he grows up.”
Anurag said, “I will like to be an electronics engineer when I grow up.”
Anurag says, “I like to be an electronics engineer when I grow up.”
Anurag says, “I would like to be an electronics engineer when I grow up.”

questions.correct_option_text: Anurag says, “I would like to be an electronics engineer when I grow up.”

questions.question_text_full:
Select the most appropriate direct form of the given sentence.
The policeman told the inspector that he had arrested a man for breaking the lock-down rules.

options.text: The policeman said to the inspector, “He arrests a man for breaking the lock-down rules.”
The policeman said to the Inspector, “He would arrest a man for breaking the lock-down rules.”
The policeman said to the inspector, “I am arresting a man for breaking the lock-down rules.”
The policeman said to the inspector, “I have arrested a man for breaking the lock-down rules.”

questions.correct_option_text: The policeman said to the inspector, “I have arrested a man for breaking the lock-down rules.”
